audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Leviticus / Vayiqra covenant blessing-and-warning text tradition, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for idols/Sabbath/sanctuary, agricultural blessing, peace, Divine dwelling, escalating covenant warnings, siege and exile trauma, land Sabbath, confession, covenant remembrance, and anti-abuse safeguards.

verses.restored: You shall not make idols for yourselves, nor raise carved image or pillar, nor put figured stone in your land to bow down upon it, for I am YHWH your Divine.
My Sabbaths you shall keep, and my sanctuary you shall revere. I am YHWH.
If in my statutes you walk and my commands you keep and do them,
then I will give your rains in their season, land shall give its produce, and trees of field shall give their fruit.
Your threshing shall reach grape harvest, and grape harvest shall reach sowing; you shall eat your bread to satisfaction and dwell securely in your land.
I will give peace in land, and you shall lie down with none making you afraid. I will remove harmful beast from land, and sword shall not pass through your land.
You shall pursue your enemies, and they shall fall before you by sword.
Five from you shall pursue a hundred, and a hundred from you shall pursue ten thousand; your enemies shall fall before you by sword.
I will turn toward you, make you fruitful, multiply you, and establish my covenant with you.
You shall eat old stored produce and bring out old because of new.
I will set my dwelling-place in your midst, and my soul/life shall not reject you.
I will walk in your midst and be your Divine, and you shall be my people.
I am YHWH your Divine, who brought you out from land of Egypt, from being their slaves; I broke bars of your yoke and made you walk upright.
But if you do not listen to me and do not do all these commands,
and if you reject my statutes, and if your soul/life loathes my judgments so you do not do all my commands, breaking my covenant,

then I too will do this to you: appoint over you terror, wasting disease, and fever consuming eyes and causing soul/life to languish; you shall sow your seed for nothing, and your enemies shall eat it.
I will set my face against you, and you shall be struck before your enemies; those hating you shall rule over you, and you shall flee when none pursues.
If after these you do not listen to me, I will add to discipline you sevenfold for your sins/misalignments.
I will break pride of your strength and make your heavens like iron and your earth like bronze.
Your strength shall be spent for nothing; your land shall not give its produce and trees of land shall not give their fruit.
If you walk contrary to me and are unwilling to listen to me, I will add plague upon you sevenfold according to your sins/misalignments.
I will send beast of field among you; it shall bereave you, cut off your livestock, diminish you, and your roads shall become desolate.
If by these you are not corrected toward me but walk contrary to me,
then I too will walk contrary to you and strike you, I also, sevenfold for your sins/misalignments.
I will bring upon you sword avenging covenant; you shall gather into your cities, and I will send pestilence in your midst, and you shall be given into hand of enemy.
When I break staff of bread for you, ten women shall bake your bread in one oven and return your bread by weight; you shall eat and not be satisfied.
If by this you do not listen to me but walk contrary to me,
then I will walk contrary to you in fury, and I also will discipline you sevenfold for your sins/misalignments.
You shall eat flesh of your sons, and flesh of your daughters you shall eat.
I will destroy your high places, cut down your incense altars, put your corpses upon corpses of your idols, and my soul/life shall reject you.
I will make your cities a ruin and desolate your sanctuaries, and I will not smell your soothing scents.
I myself will desolate land, and your enemies who dwell in it shall be appalled at it.
You I will scatter among nations, and I will unsheathe sword after you; your land shall be desolation and your cities ruin.
Then land shall enjoy its Sabbaths all days of its desolation, while you are in land of your enemies; then land shall rest and enjoy its Sabbaths.
All days of its desolation it shall rest, what it did not rest in your Sabbaths when you dwelt upon it.

As for those remaining among you, I will bring faintness into their hearts in lands of their enemies; sound of driven leaf shall pursue them, and they shall flee as from sword and fall when none pursues.
They shall stumble one over another as before sword, though no pursuer; and there shall be no standing for you before your enemies.
You shall perish among nations, and land of your enemies shall consume you.
Those remaining among you shall waste away in their iniquity in lands of your enemies; and also in iniquities of their ancestors with them they shall waste away.
Then they shall confess their iniquity and iniquity of their ancestors, in their unfaithfulness that they acted unfaithfully against me, and also that they walked contrary to me.
I too walked contrary to them and brought them into land of their enemies; then their uncircumcised heart shall be humbled, and then they shall accept their iniquity.
Then I will remember my covenant with Jacob, and also my covenant with Isaac, and also my covenant with Abraham I will remember, and land I will remember.

The land shall be forsaken by them and shall enjoy its Sabbaths while desolate without them; and they shall accept their iniquity because they rejected my judgments and their soul/life loathed my statutes.
Yet even so, when they are in land of their enemies, I will not reject them and will not loathe them to destroy them completely, to break my covenant with them; for I am YHWH their Divine.
I will remember for them covenant of ancestors whom I brought out from land of Egypt before eyes of nations, to be their Divine. I am YHWH.
These are statutes, judgments, and Torah/instructions that YHWH set between himself and children of Israel on Mount Sinai by hand of Moses.

translation_notes: Blessing culminates not merely in prosperity but in Divine dwelling, walking among the people, and liberation-shaped uprightness.
The warning section uses escalating covenant-curse rhetoric common to ancient treaty forms; it should not be individualized into simplistic blame for every illness, disaster, or trauma.
Siege cannibalism imagery reflects catastrophic warfare and must be handled as trauma text, not threat theater.
Land Sabbath becomes a theological witness during exile: exploited land receives the rest denied to it.
The chapter does not end in abandonment. Verses 44-45 explicitly deny total rejection and reaffirm covenant remembrance in exile.
safety_note: Do not use Leviticus 26 to tell abused, ill, disabled, poor, traumatized, colonized, or disaster-stricken people that their suffering proves Divine punishment. Preserve this as ancient communal covenant-warning rhetoric while centering confession, land rest, non-abandonment, and remembered liberation.
